aids.title: COPS for Bivariate Measures
aids.content: **C**ovariance (direction, scale-dependent), **O**rdinal uses Spearman, **P**earson (linear, scale-free), **S**catter plot (visualization). Remember: COPS investigate relationships between variables.
aids.forRemembering: The four key bivariate analysis tools

aids.title: The Umbrella Analogy
aids.content: Umbrellas and accidents are correlated (both increase on rainy days). But confiscating all umbrellas will not reduce accidents. Rain is the hidden cause of both. In ML, selecting features based only on correlation is like blaming umbrellas for accidents.

aids.title: Quick Decision: Pearson or Spearman?
aids.content: Ask two questions: (1) Is the data continuous and roughly normal? (2) Is the relationship linear? If YES to both, use Pearson. If NO to either, use Spearman. Spearman is the safer default when in doubt.
